data.title: Apple Stock Falls After Trump Threatens Tariffs on India-Made iPhones — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Apple shares were dropping on Friday after President Donald Trump threatened a 25% tariff on its iPhones sold in the U.S. unless they are made stateside.

"I have long ago informed Tim Cook of Apple that I expect their iPhone's that will be sold in the United States of America will be manufactured and built in the United States, not India, or anyplace else," Trump said in a post on his social-media platform, Truth Social.

"If that is not the case, a Tariff of at least 25% must be paid by Apple to the U.S.," the president wrote.

Apple shares were down 2.6% in premarket trading.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Polystyrene (PS) export prices roll over in the United States
data.description: This week, PS export prices were left unchanged in the US.An industry source in the U.S. informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices for spot export polystyrene (PS) remained unchanged during the week that concluded, while spot import prices for expandable polystyrene (EPS) experienced an increase. The stability of PS prices was noted across major global regions, including Asia, Africa, and Europe, where consistent supply and demand fundamentals contributed to a balanced market. In the upstream sector, the benzene market also exhibited stability, amidst discussions regarding decreased import volumes into the US, which assisted in sustaining equilibrium in feedstock pricing. Within the PS market, industry participants observed a potential opportunity arising due to escalating freight rates from Asia, which could render alternative sourcing regions more competitive and alter trade dynamics in the near future. GPPS export prices were assessed at the USD 1495-1515/mt FAS Houston levels while HIPS prices were assessed at the USD 1560-1580/mt FAS Houston mark, both steady from the previous week.Feedstock SM spot prices were assessed at the 46.40-46.50 cents/lb FOB US Gulf levels, a drop of (-0.90 cents/lb) last week.Meanwhile in Asia, GPPS prices were assessed flat at the USD 1100-1160/mt, up USD (+20/mt) from the previous week. HIPS prices were assessed at the USD 1200-1270/mt CFR Far East Asia levels, a week on week spike of USD (+30/mt).

data.title: Paraxylene (PX) prices left unchanged in the United States
data.description: PX prices were assessed steady in the US on Thursday.An industry source in the US on condition of anonymity informed a Polymerupdate team member, "Prices remained steady amid a softened purchase pulse in the regional markets. PX prices were assessed at the USD 875-885/mt FOB US Gulf mark, unchanged from Wednesday's assessed levels.Upstream MX prices were assessed at the 276.00-276.10 cents/gallon FOB US Gulf levels, a day on day fall of (-1 cent/gallon).Meanwhile in Asia, PX prices on Thursday were assessed at the USD 800-810/mt FOB Korea levels, down USD (-10/mt) from Wednesday.

data.title: US Stocks Brief: President Trump Also Now Threatening a 50% Tariff on the European Union Starting on June 1, 2025
Caustic soda prices quote flat in the United States
data.description: This week, caustic soda prices were left unchanged in the US.An industry source in the US informed a Polymerupdate team member, "The prices of caustic soda in the United States have remained stable, attributed to a combination of steady supply and low demand across significant end-use sectors. Producers have upheld consistent operating rates at chlor-alkali facilities, aligning their output with domestic consumption to prevent inventory surpluses. This disciplined approach to production, along with the absence of major outages or weather-related disruptions, has contributed to market stability. On the demand front, consumption from key downstream sectors such as alumina refining, pulp and paper, and water treatment has not exhibited any notable fluctuations. Seasonal demand increases, especially from water treatment applications, have been moderate and inadequate to exert upward pressure on prices. At the same time, industrial consumption continues to encounter challenges due to broader economic uncertainty and cautious procurement practices. The source added, "Export activity has contributed to the stability of prices. Although US caustic soda is competitively priced in the international market, particularly in Latin America and certain regions of Asia, export volumes have remained consistent rather than experiencing a surge. Logistical challenges, such as ongoing issues with container availability and freight costs, have hindered any significant increase in overseas shipments that might lead to domestic supply constraints. Furthermore, the chlorine market, which is directly associated with caustic soda as a co-product, has maintained a relatively balanced state. With no significant fluctuations in chlorine demand or pricing, chlor-alkali producers have had minimal motivation to aggressively adjust caustic soda prices in order to manage overall margins. In conclusion, the trend of flat pricing in the US caustic soda market is supported by stable production levels, subdued demand growth, balanced export flows, and a stable chlorine market. Unless there is a notable shift in domestic industrial activity or global trade conditions, prices are anticipated to remain predominantly stable in the near future.On Friday, spot US Gulf caustic soda prices were assessed at the USD 470-480/mt FOB levels, rolled over from last week.Meanwhile in Asia, caustic soda prices on Friday were assessed at the USD 400-410/mt FOB NEA levels, stable week on week.In Europe, caustic soda prices on Friday were assessed at the USD 520-530/mt FOB Rotterdam levels, a fall of USD (-5/mt) from the previous week.

data.title: US Dollar Falls Early Friday Ahead of Holiday Weekend
data.description:
The US dollar fell against its major trading partners early Friday before the beginning of the long US Memorial Day holiday weekend.

Kansas City Federal Reserve President Jeffrey Schmid is due to speak at 9:35 am ET, followed by new home sales data for April at 10:00 am ET and the Kansas City Fed's services reading for May at 11:00 am ET.

St. Louis Fed is due to update its Q2 gross domestic product Nowcast estimate around midday and Fed Governor Lisa Cook is due to speak at 12:00 pm ET.

A quick summary of foreign exchange activity heading into Friday:

rose to 1.1349 from 1.1279 at the Thursday US close and 1.1293 at the same time Thursday morning. There are no Eurozone data on Friday's schedule, but European Central Bank Chief Economist Philip Lane said earlier in the day he expects slower services inflation growth later in the year, according to Reuters. ECB policy board member Isabel Schnabel is due to speak at 12:00 pm ET. The next European Central Bank meeting is scheduled for June 4-5.

rose to 1.3495 from 1.3419 at the Thursday US close and 1.3406 at the same time Thursday morning. UK retail sales rose much faster than expected according to data released overnight. The next Bank of England meeting is scheduled for June 19.

fell to 143.0903 from 143.9854 at the Thursday US close and 143.6586 at the same time Thursday morning. Japanese consumer price growth accelerated in April according to data released overnight. The next Bank of Japan meeting is scheduled for June 16-17.

fell to 1.3813 from 1.3865 at the Thursday US close and 1.3864 at the same time Thursday morning. Canada retail sales data for March are due to be released at 8:30 am ET. The next Bank of Canada meeting is scheduled for June 4.

data.title: BMO on The Day Ahead in Canada
data.description:
Canada will get the first glimpse into spending in a tariff world when the country's March retail sales are released at 8:30 a.m. ET Friday, said Bank of Montreal (BMO).

The bank is looking for a 0.7% month-over-month gain, in line with the flash estimate, but the figure will be flattered by front-running ahead of auto tariffs — implemented in early April). That suggests spending excluding autos could decline for the first time since November.

One potential bright spot is that volumes are expected to be firmer than the headline, as goods prices were softer in the month, stated BMO.

The bank will also be watching the April flash estimate, which will capture the current United States-Canada tariff environment.

The US dollar (USD) is weaker early Friday (BBDXY -0.50%), with the Canadian dollar (CAD or loonie) stronger (CAD per USD -0.34%), added BMO.

data.title: Trade, Geopolitics Caps European Bourses Midday
data.description:
European bourses largely tracked sideways midday Friday as traders await for the next shoes to drop in trade wars, and conflicts in the Middle East and Eastern Europe.

Oil stocks gained, while retail issues lagged on continental trading floors.

Investors also eyed Wall Street futures marginally signaling red, and uneven closes overnight on Asian exchanges.

In economic news, UK retail sales volumes in April rose 1.2% from March and were up 5% on year, reported Office for National Statistics.

The pan-continental Stoxx Europe 600 Index was steady mid-session.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Technology Index was flat, and the Stoxx 600 Banks Index lost 0.1%.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Oil and Gas Index was up 0.8%, but the Stoxx 600 Europe Food and Beverage Index declined 0.1%.

The REITE, a European REIT index, was flat, but the Stoxx Europe 600 Retail Index declined 0.5%.

On the national market indexes, Germany's DAX was up 0.1%, and the FTSE 100 in London was flat. The CAC 40 in Paris was off 0.5%, and Spain's IBEX 35 was stable.

Yields on benchmark 10-year German bonds were stable, near 2.62%.

Front-month North Sea Brent crude-oil futures were up 0.1% to $64.47 per barrel.

The Euro Stoxx 50 volatility index was down 0.2% to 18.32, indicating below-average volatility for European stock markets in the next 30 days, a positive signal. A reading above 20 indicates choppier markets ahead, while below 20 suggests calmer exchanges.

data.title: Perpetua Resources Up 3% in U.S. Pre-market on Application to Increase Potential Funding for Stibnite Gold Project in Idaho
data.description:
Perpetua Resources traded 3.3% higher at last look Friday in Nasdaq pre-market trading as the company submitted an application to the Export-Import Bank of the United States to increase potential debt financing for the Stibnite gold project in Idaho to US$2 billion from US$1.8 billion.

The company said the increase in potential funding reflects the higher estimated number of job-years indicated by the financial update and basic engineering work completed in the first quarter. The bank extended a non-binding letter of interest to Perpetua in April 2024 for the US$1.8 billion. If approved, the debt financing could fund a majority of the project's development costs to become the only domestically mined source of antimony, the company said.

"The Stibnite gold project is poised to be a national strategic asset for domestic antimony production and is also a world class gold asset," said Jon Cherry, president and CEO of Perpetua Resources. "EXIM financing could play a pivotal role in advancing the project to production so we can reestablish a secure supply of antimony for the U.S. for decades to come."

data.title: Perpetua Resources Seeks $2 Billion in EXIM Debt Financing to Advance Stibnite Gold-Antimony Project
data.description:
Perpetua Resources said Friday it had formally applied to the US Export-Import Bank for up to $2 billion in debt financing to build its Stibnite Gold Project in Idaho.

The company said the application follows the project's final federal permit approval and ongoing engagement with the Export-Import Bank's underwriting team.

Perpetua Resources said the debt financing would cover most development costs and help establish a domestic source of antimony - a key mineral for national defense - amid tightening Chinese export restrictions.

EXIM will now begin its standard due diligence and eligibility review process, it added.

data.title: Michelin on track to generate a fifth of sales from non-tyre business by 2030
data.description:
Michelin is on track to generate 20% of its revenue outside of the tyre business by 2030, an executive said on Friday, as the company diversifies away from a core business pummelled by major shifts in the global car industry.

The French firm is one of the world's top tyre producers but is facing severe competition from Chinese rivals with lower production costs, and a shrinking client base in Europe.

It currently generates about 15-16% of revenues from non-core activities like services and composite materials and is aiming to grow this through both organic growth and acquisitions, said Maude Portigliatti, director of the group's Business Polymer Composite Solutions unit, in a press briefing.

As part of this growth, Portigliatti also announced a 60 million euro ($68.07 million) investment in a factory near Lyon to produce 5-HMF, a biobased molecule that can replace the use of fossil fuel-derived formaldehyde in resins and glues as well as other wide-ranging uses.

The plant, with an annual capacity of 3,000 tons, will start in 2026 and is expected to become the largest production site for 5-HMF in the world.

Analysts at Jefferies upgraded Michelin on Friday to a "buy" rating, pointing to its better earnings growth relative to peers and lower exposure to autos, which they say carry risks in 2025.

($1 = 0.8814 euros)

data.title: Hisamitsu Pharmaceutical to Cancel 10 Million Shares of Treasury Stock
data.description:
Hisamitsu Pharmaceutical will cancel 10 million shares of treasury stock on June 12, as approved by its Board of Directors, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The cancellation represents 13.70% of the total issued shares before cancellation, excluding treasury stock.

Following the cancellation, the company's total outstanding shares will be 75,164,895.
